messages.content: where i can find the page cloudstore

You can access the Firebase console at https://console.firebase.google.com/ and follow these steps to access Cloud Firestore:

1. Go to your project dashboard.
2. Click on the "Firestore Database" option in the left-hand menu.
3. You should now be able to see and manage your Cloud Firestore database. 

From there, you can create collections and documents, set up security rules, and more. Make sure you have the necessary permissions to access and modify Firestore in your Firebase project.

[Error: Objects are not valid as a React child (found: object with keys {seconds, nanoseconds}). If you meant to render a collection of children, use an array instead.]
AAAA i cant what do i do?

This error occurs when you try to render an object that React doesn't know how to display. In your case, you're trying to render an object that contains two properties: "seconds" and "nanoseconds". To fix this, you should convert the object to a displayable string before rendering it in your component.

One way to convert the object to a string is to use the "toDate()" function, which converts the object to a Date object, and then use a date formatting library like moment.js to format the date into a displayable string.

Here's an example of how you can do this:

```
import moment from 'moment'; // import moment.js library

// Inside your component, use moment.js to format the date from "seconds" and "nanoseconds"
<Text style={gStyle.dateFutureMK}>
  {moment(mkData.time.toDate()).format('MMM Do, YYYY')} // format the date into a displayable string
</Text>
```

In this example, we assume "mkData.time" is the object containing "seconds" and "nanoseconds". We first use the ".toDate()" function to convert it to a Date object, and then use moment.js to format it into a displayable string. This will prevent the React error from occurring.

Make sure to install moment.js in your project by running "npm install moment --save" in your command line.
It works! but i dont see pics

If you're not seeing the images displayed, there are several things you can check:

1. Make sure that you have set up Firebase Storage correctly and that the images are uploaded to your Firebase Storage Bucket. You can check this by going to the Firebase console and navigating to Storage.

2. Check that the URLs for the images are correct and that they're being stored in the "imageUrl" property in the "FutureMK" collection.

3. Make sure that you have the correct Firebase configuration set up in your project, with the correct API keys and other necessary configurations. You can refer to the Firebase documentation on how to set this up.

4. Ensure that your app has the appropriate permissions to access the internet. To do this, you should add the following line in your app's AndroidManifest.xml file if you're targeting Android:

```
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
```

or the following setting in the Info.plist file if you're targeting iOS:

```
<key>NSAppTransportSecurity</key>
<dict>
  <key>NSAllowsArbitraryLoads</key>
  <true/>
</dict>
```

If none of these solutions fix the issue, you could try debugging the problem by logging the image URLs to the console and checking that they're correct, or testing the URLs directly in a browser.

RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded
what does that mean? i used image instesd of imageUrl

This error typically occurs when a function is calling itself repeatedly and exceeding the maximum stack size for the call stack. This can happen when there's an infinite loop or recursion within your code.

In your case, the error might be occurring because you're trying to render an image using the `Image` component and passing `props.image` directly as the `source` prop. 

To fix this error, make sure that you're passing a valid image URL as the `uri` value in the `source` prop. If you're using an object to represent the image in your data (e.g., `mkData.image`), make sure that it has the `uri` property set to the correct image URL.

Here's an example of how to use the `Image` component to display an image:

```
import { Image } from 'react-native';

// ...

// use Image component to display the image
<Image source={{ uri: mkData.image.uri }} style={/* your image style */} />
```

Make sure to replace `mkData.image.uri` with the valid URI for your image.

Also, to prevent the maximum call stack size exceeded error, make sure that you're not calling any recursive functions and that your code is not causing an infinite loop. You can use a debugger or console logs to identify any potential issues related to this.

If you're still not seeing any images displayed, make sure to check the following:

1. Check that the URLs for the images in the "FutureMK" collection are correct and that they're being stored in the "imageUrl" property. You can check this by going to the Firebase console and inspecting the "FutureMK" collection.

2. Make sure that the image URLs are accessible from your app. This means ensuring that the images are publicly accessible, or that your Firebase Storage rules allow read access for all users.

3. Verify that there are no typos or errors in the "uri" value that you're passing to the `Image` component's `source` prop. Make sure that the URI is valid and that it points to an actual image.

4. Check if there are any console warnings or errors that might be related to the images not showing up. These can provide valuable hints on what's going wrong.

If you've checked all these points and still can't figure out why the images aren't showing up, try debugging the issue by checking the network requests in your browser's developer tools or by inspecting the image component's properties using React Native Debugger.
well i upload pics from storage firebase but there is no pics

If you're uploading images to Firebase Storage but they're not showing up in your app, there could be several reasons:

1. Make sure that you're uploading the images to the correct Firebase Storage Bucket. You can check that the images are uploaded by going to the Firebase console and navigating to Storage.

2. Verify that you're using the correct path to access the images in your app. Double-check the URLs that you're using in your `Image` component's `source` prop. Make sure to use the correct path to the image, which includes the bucket name and any folders or subdirectories where the image is located.

3. Check that your Firebase Storage rules allow public reads. You can check and modify your storage rules in the Firebase console. Make sure that you have set up the rules to allow anyone to read the images.

4. Check the formats of the images you are using, the supported file formats like PNG, JPEG, and GIF.

If none of these solutions work, try verifying that you are correctly uploading the data to the correct collection in your Firestore database.
